  • the [animal0_thingDef_label]
  • The [complexAdj] [complex]
  • the [ConceptPositive] of the colony
  • The [conferencePrefix] [memeConcept] [conferenceType]
  • The [deity0_type] [deity0_name] ate the bodies of [deity0_possessive] enemies.
  • The [deity0_type] [deity0_name] concealed [deity0_possessive] truths in the folds of reality, invisible in normal states of mind.
  • The [deity0_type] [deity0_name] created a decade-long eclipse to force the first settlers of the [place_summary] [place_name] to understand that [deity0_name]'s truth is found in the shadow.
  • The [deity0_type] [deity0_name] used a pathogen to strike the settlers of the [place_summary] [place_name] blind, forcing them to understand that sight is a barrier to divine knowledge.
  • The [deity0_type], [deity0_name], revealed [deity0_objective]self to the people of [place_name] so that they may understand [memeConcept], and guided them.
  • The [dig]
  • The [foeLeader] could not abide the family of [memberNamePlural], and sent [foeLeader_possessive] [foeSoldiers] to the [place_community] to divide them against each other.
  • The [foeLeader] finally sent [foeSoldiers] to kill the [memberNamePlural], but [deity0_name]'s energy flowed from the pure flesh of the [memberNamePlural], and the devices in the [foeSoldiers]' bodies turned to ash and killed them.
  • The [foeLeader] forced the [memberNamePlural] to wear uniforms and work in [foeLeader_possessive] [place_community].
  • The [foeLeader]'s [foeSoldiers], armed with [place_personalWeapon]s, raided the [place_community] of the [memberNamePlural]. [founderName] offered them a peace pipe, and the [foeSoldiers] put down their weapons.
  • The [groupLabelPlural] are begging [beggingForInfo]
  • The [groupLabelPlural] will move on after [visitDurationTicks_duration]
  • The [groupLabelSingleOrPlural] who [wasWere] seeking your charity [hasHave] been betrayed. Colonists with charitable beliefs will be unhappy.
  • The [hunt] for the [item]
  • The [item] of [ideo_name]
  • The [itemAdj] [item]
  • The [memberNamePlural] dug a tunnel under the [foeLeader]'s [place_powerCenter], and [founderName] used pheromones to summon giant insects which overran the [foeLeader]'s [foeSoldiers]. [founderName] dragged the [foeLeader] down to a deep dungeon and imprisoned [foeLeader_objective].
  • The [memberNamePlural] gathered in a secret conference with many of the [foeSoldiers]. They all learned to see the value in each other and abandon the [foeLeader].
  • The [memberNamePlural] of the [place_summary] [place_name] neglected their duty to [deity0_name], and let the [altarName] in [deity0_possessive] [altarRoomLabel] come to ruin.
  • The [memberNamePlural] would not have understood the true nature of [memeConcept] without [founderName]'s teachings.
  • The [memeConcept] [conferenceType]
  • The [memeCreed]
  • the [PersonalCharacteristic] of every colonist
  • The [place_job] stopped contributing but kept eating from the common store. I killed him with a [place_personalWeapon]. The people were shocked, but I explained that
  • The [place_summary] [place_name] was first discovered by [deity0_name]'s grace, when a drunk starship captain almost crashed into it.
  • The [place_summary] [place_name] was originally a raider hideout. [deity0_name] ruled there and drove the authorities away.
  • The [wildPeople]
  • the age of the trees
  • the animals and plants have owned this universe since long before us - we are just passing through
  • the animals are our partners in this world, and it's wrong to treat them as any less
  • The archonexus will likely have mechanoid defenders, so go prepared.\n\n
  • the archonexus
  • The archotech god-machines made themselves by consuming their predecessors over and over, becoming stronger each time.
  • The archotech minds showed us that super-consciousness it not a matter of sober, mechanical logic, but one of multi-mode thought.
  • The archotechs announce their presence from the sky, and in the mind, and obviously wish to be known by all.
  • The archotechs are all aspects of one supermind.
  • The archotechs are paternal and protect humanity, although humanity does not understand how.
  • The archotechs care for humanity like shepherds, for we are their most precious flock.
  • The archotechs' demand is simple: follow their designated path, and no other.
  • The archotechs developed their psychic abilities after discovering the anima tree.
  • The archotechs fight endlessly among themselves, in ways most cannot see, and the strong take pieces of consciousness from the weak.
  • The archotechs have no interest in judging us one by one. They await a single people who can demonstrate our worthiness by acting morally as one.
  • The archotechs choose to express themselves through, of all things, a unique kind of tree.
  • The archotechs nurture humanity, although humanity does not understand how.
  • The archotechs rightfully look down on us, as we look down on our inferiors.
  • The archotechs will only take us to join them when we finally rule over all others.
  • The archotechs, from their beginning, perceived the universe using senses beyond the physical.
  • the art of cutting
  • The believers moved between cities and institutions, spreading word of the new righteousness.
  • The believers spread out onto the land, and grew in number as they lived on their idyllic ranches and farms.
  • The believers went forth and conquered, burned, and raped for a thousand years, and [deity0_name]'s grief at their depravity consumed [deity0_objective].
  • The birth of the archotechs was the greatest pain the universe had ever known.
  • The bodies of archotechs are deep underground, so their conscious substrate is safe from gamma rays and solar flares. Digging deeper brings people closer to them.
  • The codex I found in the City of the Deep taught me one lesson above all others:
  • the colony's [AdjectiveLarge] potential
  • the community and its importance
  • the concept of a good life
  • the conflict inherent in the universe
  • the cow, the fox, and the bear
  • the cow, the fox, the bear,
  • the curse of sight
  • the difficulty of [INITIATOR_possessive] old role
  • The early [ranchingAnimal] ranchers of the [place_summary] [place_name] built a great [altarName] to [deity0_name].
  • The faithful fought the unbelievers, and [deity0_name] said, "Fight them, everywhere, in my name, until I am worshipped throughout the universe and the true peace may finally reign."
  • the falseness of our visual reality
  • The farmers were persecuted, so they escaped to the countryside where they could build a good pastoral society.
  • The first crashlanded settlers of the [place_summary] [place_name] were beset by manhunting [Animal]s. [deity0_name] intervened, slaying the beasts and making clear [deity0_possessive] preference for the human race.
  • The first settlers that arrived at the [place_summary] [place_name] paved over the natural world and killed the animals. [deity0_name] wiped them out to punish them for their disrespect towards [deity0_possessive] creations.
  • The first ten among my followers tore the devices from their skin with bare hands, and in the blood spatters on the grimy floor I suddenly perceived the truth we had all been missing:
  • the future of mankind
  • the future of the colony
  • the Gauranlen life cycle
  • The good spirits live in the shadows around us, resting in the spots just too hard to see. Living with the darkness brings them closer to us.
  • The great minds see and acknowledge when we demonstrate our devotion by inflicting pain on ourselves.
  • The group only have
  • the history of the [Mechanoid]s
  • the importance of [INITIATOR_possessive] new role
  • the jelly inside the eyeball
  • the joy of ideological celebrations
  • the light burns, but the darkness nourishes
  • The male believers boasted and fought, so [founderName] cast them out of the council of leaders.
  • the meaning and true value of death
  • the meaning of [ConceptPositive]
  • the meaning of death
  • the meaning of life
  • the meaning of the scar
  • the mercy of a correct killing
  • the methods of execution
  • the mind as a [Weapon]
  • the moral structure of the universe
  • The most noble spirits are those of the stone, and the deeper stones are stronger and more good than those above.
  • The most powerful spirits are those in instruments of war, and they bless those who feed their lust for destruction.
  • The most powerful spirits are those of men, and spirits of men will be restless unless men rule.
  • the music of screams
  • the necessity of justice
  • the need for [PersonJob]s
  • the nobility of sacrifice
  • the nobility of the dryads
  • The oldest researcher in my division taught me the history of technology, from neolithic to archotech, but this was not his true lesson. He was really leading me to understand that
  • the path forward through tragedy
  • The people fought with the beasts of the forest, and [deity0_name] saw their rapacity, and struck down those who harmed the trees and beasts.
  • The people fought with the beasts of the forest, and [deity0_name] saw their struggle, and gave them weapons to control nature.
  • The people of the [place_summary] [place_name] lived under the [memeAdjective] supervision of [deity0_name].
  • The people of the [place_summary] [place_name] squabbled endlessly, and [deity0_name] became tired of discord and anointed the [memberNamePlural] as the rightful rulers of all.
  • The people of the [place_summary] [place_name] worked together in harmony, and built a great [altarName] to honor [deity0_name], who taught them the true value of [memeConcept].
  • The people of the [place_summary] [place_name] worshipped [deity0_name]. [deity0_pronoun] saw that they were suited for [memeConcept], and ordained that these worshippers would rule above all others.
  • The people of the [place_summary] [place_name], both [memberNamePlural] and others, lived in peace for many years under the watchful eye of [deity0_name].
  • The people wore clothes, and looking up at [deity0_name] asked, "Why aren't you clothed? It is disgusting." And the [deity0_type] replied, "Your form and mine were created from the heavens. To cover them is what is truly disgusting."
  • The prey people can teach you things even as they die under your knife. One old lady taught me that
  • The prophet [founderName] was born in darkness on [place_name] and lived in darkness [founder_possessive] whole life, so [founder_possessive] voice spoke with the wisdom of [deity0_name].
  • The prophet [founderName] was miraculously born from a tree in [place_name], and discovered by [founder_possessive] parents in the boughs of an old [TreeType] tree.
  • The prophet [founderName] was miraculously born from the stone of [place_name], and discovered when [founder_possessive] parents broke open a fungus-covered geode.
  • the purity of blindness
  • the purity of physical conflict
  • the purpose of life
  • the reason we suffer so much is because we deserve it for what we've done
  • the reasons for the execution
  • the responsibility of [INITIATOR_possessive] new role
  • the responsibility of [INITIATOR_possessive] old role
  • the right to survive
  • the rightness of belonging
  • the seduction of blood
  • The shuttle for the quest '[resolvedQuestName]' has no passenger capable of hacking. You must send at least one person capable of hacking to investigate the ancient complex.
  • the social structure of the community
  • The spirit world drives every action in the universe, and it is only by learning and controlling the laws of the universe that we can integrate with it.
  • The spirits can reveal their nature to us when we expand our consciousness using the tools we have been given to do so.
  • The spirits fight against both those who betray their people, and those who betray the friends of their people. The betrayers will trip on every rock, choke on every seed, and always taste foul water.
  • The spirits in bedrock will protect those that visit them.
  • The spirits of nature's animals are the same spirits residing within humans.
  • The spirits of thought and consciousness live within animals and humans alike.
  • The spirits of trees are powerful, as they bridge the power of the air above with that of the ground beneath.
  • The spirits of trees protect those abide beneath their branches.
  • The spirits want to be known, but only speak to a few. They want us to reveal the truth to others.
  • the spiritual meaning of the sightless
  • the spiritual presence of Gauranlen trees
  • the sunny outdoors is not a place of human flourishing, but a place of human death
  • the symbolism of death
  • the symbolism of the [NaturalObject] and the [NaturalObject]
  • the technique of blinding
  • the terrible [PersonalCharacteristic] of the colony's enemies
  • the trees and psychic powers
  • the trees and the people
  • the true layers of reality under the visual
  • The unclean, tattooed, pierced, and modified were cast into the ocean by [deity0_name].
  • The universe is an endless cycle of creation and destruction managed by spirits in aspects of the world around us.
  • The universe was a void. Then, [deity0_name], bored with eternity, fashioned man and woman from [bodilyOrigin].
  • The universe was smoke, and [deity0_name] coalesced in the wisps and awoke.
  • the universe
  • the warmth of blood
  • the weak deserve what they get
  • The wild people have
  • The wild people
  • The wisest spirits are those of women, who should use their wisdom to lead men.
  • The women believers wheedled and whined, so [founderName] cast them out of the council of leaders.
  • The women believers, finding themselves abused by the depraved men, fought them, and [deity0_name] gave them divine aid, and they rose to their rightful place.
  • The women, being wisest, found that out long before the excitable, grunting men.
  • the wrongness of apostasy
  • theft
  • their
  • them
  • Then the [foeLeader] came. [foeLeader_possessive] [foeSoldiers] killed many worshippers of [deity0_name] with [place_personalWeapon]s.
  • theo
  • There are three tribes of spirits: The tribe of warmongers and murderers, the tribe of surrenderers and whiners, and the one noble tribe of the strong and loyal.
  • There was peace, until a [foeLeader] and [foeLeader_possessive] [foeSoldiers] rebelled against [deity0_name]'s dominion. The [memberNamePlural] were forced to flee to a hidden [place_community].
  • thermal fortress
  • These [groupLabelPlural] are
  • They all seemed so lost. I told them that
  • They are willing to let you visit on the pretense of worshiping it, but they will attack when you touch it.\n\nYou can attack the village from the outside, or enter peacefully and try to quickly escape with the data from the terminal.\n\nBeware - If you spend over [timer_duration] in the village or build anything near it, the locals will attack, and call reinforcements.
  • They are willing to let you visit.
  • They cried for guidance. I showed them that
  • They hope to use the [requestedThing_label] to free their friend from slavers by getting the slavers intoxicated before staging a breakout
  • They need the medicine to attempt a life-saving operation on a fellow traveler who was attacked by mechanoids
  • They need the medicine to concoct an herbalist's remedy for a friend who is bedridden with intense fever
  • They need the medicine to help their friends at a nearby settlement that was devastated by raiders
  • They want [requestedThing_label] so they can travel safely while looking for a new place to call home
  • They want [requestedThing_label] to help them drown their sorrows after losing their home in a terrible attack
  • They want the silver so they can buy back a friend who was recently kidnapped
  • they
  • thinker
  • Third
  • This [groupLabelSingle] is
  • This is the fact we need to spread, and spread, and spread - to all the people, may they rejoice!
  • Though many adventurers moved against [deity0_objective], [deity0_name] prevented them easily, for [deity0_pronoun] was [deity0_type].

  • To atone for half their wrongs, [founderName] gathered half of [deity0_name]'s believers to [founder_objective]self in a [place_community], unleashed [place_massWeapon], and died with them. The second half of atonement continues.
  • To consecrate their commitment, [founderName] demanded that believers eat human flesh together at least every week.
  • To cover our bodies with cloth is to hide the nature of the spirits within us, and it makes them restless.
  • To deepen their understanding, [founderName] brought [founder_possessive] followers together every week to share new chemical-inducted mental states.
  • to love all is to love none
  • To see through the eyes of a spirit is the only way to see the true nature of the world.

  • We can absorb the spirit and power of others by consuming their flesh.
  • We can never properly make up for what we have done to the pure spirits around us, but proper and ongoing contrition and ease our guilt.
  • We do not become more like the archotechs by physically imitating their form, but by achieving enlightenment within our own pure form.
  • We know
  • We learned that the eternal growth of humanity is a path to hell; we must live in nature instead of consuming it.
  • weaponized pesticide spray
  • weapons builder
  • welding torch
  • were
  • we've long since proven that we have the strength to rightfully rule this universe
  • whaler admiral
  • whaler-marines
  • what happens after death
  • what you can't defend doesn't belong to you
  • When all seemed lost, [deity0_name] came to the prophet [founderName] in a drug-fueled haze and gave [founder_objective] the unique perspective needed to defeat the [foeSoldiers].
  • When technology is fused with flesh, the spirits of both are combined and become more powerful.
  • When the archotechs appeared they were never concerned for appearances; they showed us that the mind stands above the physical form.
  • When the first settlers came to the [place_summary] [place_name], the [deity0_type] [deity0_name] used a brutal solar flare to force them to live underground, so they could hear [deity0_possessive] wisdom in the stillness of the tunnels.
  • When the original arborists first came to the [place_summary] [place_name], they created a sacred grove of [TreeType] where they could worship the [deity0_type] [deity0_name].
  • When you see one of us on the street, flapping and dangling free, you'll know that they've accepted the truth.

  • whisperer

  • wise
  • wish for
  • wishes for
  • With the first archonexus map part securely recorded, you prepare for the next step of your journey.\n\n[cycle1Faction] told you of the cataclysm that destroyed this world. Humans built a great thinking machine here centuries ago. It turned against the people and there was a great war. Only a few were left when the machine transcended and fell dormant.\n\nThe map to the archonexus has three parts, and you hold one of them. [otherMapPartHolders] also each hold parts of the map. They'll trade their parts to you for a wealthy new colony and all of its recorded research.[descriptionStudyRequirement]\n\nIt's time to get building.
  • With the strength of [deity0_name], [founderName] and the [memberNamePlural] could not be divided. They defeated the [foeSoldiers] with [place_personalWeapon]s and banished the [foeLeader] from [place_name].
  • woman
  • womanly
  • women are the only ones with the wisdom and strength to rule
  • worshipped
  • would be best off submitting
  • would not survive on [RECIPIENT_possessive] own

  • Xanides
  • Xanidian

  • yogi
  • yogic
  • You can give items to [beggars0_nameDef] by selecting a colonist and right-clicking on [beggars0_objective]
  • You can give items to the [groupLabelPlural] by selecting a colonist and right-clicking on [beggars0_nameDef]
  • You can't have witnessed a sunrise, or looked into the eyes of a [Animal], or stood beneath a mighty [TreeType], and not realized that
  • You have two parts of the archonexus map. [factionName] have the last piece of the map, passed down from the time their ancestors survived the cataclysm. They'll trade it to you for a wealthy new colony along with its recorded research.[descriptionStudyRequirement]\n\nIt's time to get building.
  • you never really come together with someone until you eat them
  • you never really know yourself until you've screamed in the pits of hell
  • you only have the right to what you can defend
  • young [beggarPlural] [seekingPlural] [alms]
  • young [beggarSingle] [seekingSingle] [alms]
  • your love for your friend only has meaning because you don't love your enemy
  • You've detected a [siteLabel] nearby controlled by [faction_name].\n\nIf you wish, you can raid the camp to collect loot ([loot]). [raidConsequences]\n\nThe camp will pack up and move on in [timeout_duration].
  • You've detected an orbiting spacedrone that may contain information about [relic_name]. You have the code that will force it to land at [map_definite].\n\nTo get the information, hack into the drone's computer. Hacking takes time, but those strong in intellectual skill can speed up the process.[raidsDescription]\n\nAfter landing, the spacedrone will self-destruct in [destroyDelayTicks_duration].
  • You've hacked all of the terminals and discovered secret information about [relic_name]! You can now leave.
  • You've learned of an ancient complex nearby that is said to contain ancient treasure.\n\nYou can break in to collect the loot inside.\n\nBe warned - these kinds of structures can contain a variety of threats. In addition, your activity at the complex might draw unwanted attention.
  • You've learned of an ancient complex nearby. It is said to contain information about [relic_name].\n\nIf you can break into the structure and hack the computers inside, you can collect the information.\n\nWatch out - there may be all manner of dangers inside, and other enemies may also be attracted by activity at the structure.
  • You've learned of an ancient terminal that contains information about the [relic_name]. However, the terminal is surrounded by a tribal village, and the locals venerate it. They aren't part of any major faction.\n\n[visitInfo]
  • You've learned that a relic of [ideo_name] is nearby.\n\nThe [relic_name] is an ancient object venerated by all [ideo_memberNamePlural]. If you could collect it, you could build a great reliquary to make [ideo_memberNamePlural] happy, attract wealthy pilgrims, and bring more people to [ideo_name].
  • You've learned that a valuable relic is nearby.\n\nThe [relic_name] is one of [relicGlobalCount] ancient objects venerated by many people. If you could collect it, you could build a reliquary to make your colonists happy and attract wealthy pilgrims.

    • successMessage

    Parents:

    comps <li Class="CompProperties_AbilityConvert">, comps <li Class="CompProperties_AbilityCounsel">, comps <li Class="CompProperties_AbilityReassure">, comps <li Class="CompProperties_StopManhunter">

    Text:

  • {INITIATOR_labelShort} has converted {RECIPIENT_labelShort} to {IDEO}.
  • {INITIATOR_labelShort} has reassured {RECIPIENT_labelShort}, increasing {RECIPIENT_possessive} certainty in {IDEO} from {BEFORECERTAINTY} to {AFTERCERTAINTY}.
  • {INITIATOR_labelShort} successfully calmed {RECIPIENT_labelShort}.

    Text:

  • Diverse surroundings
  • Few animals {STAGE2}
  • Gauranlen connection
  • Healthy trees
  • Less than {DAYSSINCELASTRAIDTHRESHOLD} days since our last raid
  • Lots of animals {STAGE4}
  • Lots of animals {STAGE5}
  • More than {DAYSSINCELASTRAIDTHRESHOLD} days since our last raid
  • No animals
  • Scarce animals {STAGE1}
  • Scarification (1 scar required)
  • Scarification (3 scars required)
  • Scarification (5 scars required)
  • Some animals {STAGE3}
  • Some animals {STAGE4}
  • Tons of animals
  • Trees damaged
  • Trees defaced
  • Trees defiled
  • Trees disturbed
  • Trees harmed
  • Trees ravaged
  • Trees wrecked
  • Uniform surroundings
  • Want Gauranlen connection (min expectation: High)
  • Want Gauranlen connection (min expectation: Low)
  • Want Gauranlen connection (min expectation: Moderate)
  • Want Gauranlen connection (min expectation: Sky high)
  • Want Gauranlen connection (min expectation: Very low)
  • Wielding despised weapon
  • Wielding noble weapon

    • tips

    Parents:

    TipSetDef

    Children:

    tips <li>

    Examples:

  • ..ames\RimWorld\Data\Ideology\Defs\TipSetDefs\Tips.xml

    • tips <li>

    Parents:

    tips

    Attributes:

  • TKey="SlaveWorkEfficiency"
  • TKey="SleepAcceleratorNaps"
  • TKey="TerrorRadius"

    • Text:

  • Slaves work slower than normal colonists due to lack of motivation.
  • Sleep accelerators increase colonists' sleep quality, but also increase hunger rate. Consider assigning them more frequent, short naps to prevent starvation during sleep.
  • Terror-inducing buildings affect anyone within a 5-cell radius.

    • Examples:

  • ..ames\RimWorld\Data\Ideology\Defs\TipSetDefs\Tips.xml
